trigger.reason: Anthropic's Claude Tag Slack integration is a new product release from a top-tier AI lab meeting criteria 1+2, with plausible market-moving potential in customer-service software, collaboration tools, and enterprise SaaS; flagged with bias-correction given Anthropic source.

primary_event.headline: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
primary_event.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a new Slack integration that embeds an AI teammate directly into Slack workspaces. The feature captures organizational context and institutional knowledge through ongoing message monitoring, positioning it as both a productivity tool and a strategic play to deepen enterprise workflow integration.

secondary_events.headline: OpenAI launches initiative to find and patch open source bugs

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Anthropic's Claude Tag Slack integration launch, an enterprise collaboration AI play with bias-correction applied for Anthropic-sourced trigger.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger targets enterprise collaboration/SaaS productivity, but the day's biggest decliners are semiconductor names (ONTO, PLAB, AMBA, AEIS, CGNX) whose business models have essentially zero overlap with a Slack AI teammate — classic sympathy-fade where 'AI lab ships product' bleeds into broad tech selloff. DOCN is the only candidate with even tangential connection (cloud productivity-adjacent), but Slack-embedded AI doesn't replace IaaS. The cybersecurity and edtech names (VRNS, QLYS, TENB, EVH, DUOL) moved UP, consistent with the market correctly recognizing they are not in the blast radius.

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss AI only as a peripheral operational risk ('use or anticipated use of new and evolving technologies, such as AI ... may increase or create new operational risks') — no mention of AI competition to their semiconductor process-control business. Core business is metrology/inspection for advanced node fabs.
discoveries.thesis: ONTO sells process-control equipment to advanced-node fabs (including AI accelerator manufacturing). A Slack AI integration has zero bearing on wafer inspection demand; if anything, more AI infrastructure buildout means more advanced-node capacity and more demand for ONTO's tools. The sharp drop is pure semi-cap sympathy on an unrelated AI headline.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued strong AI capex commentary from TSMC/Samsung/Intel; ONTO order book or backlog disclosures showing growth; Rigaku deal closing on schedule.
discoveries.what_kills: Evidence of AI capex digestion at leading-edge fabs; customer concentration risk materializing (a key customer cutting orders); Rigaku regulatory snag.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Semi-cap equipment names get dragged into every 'AI lab ships X' selloff regardless of product overlap — the sympathy fade is mechanical and tradable when the trigger has no fab-equipment connection.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: ONTO is in the 'AI-adjacent semis' basket that retail dumps on any AI-lab news, despite being a picks-and-shovels beneficiary of AI infrastructure buildout.
research_pointers: Check ONTO's most recent advanced-packaging and HBM-related commentary on last earnings call
Look at WFE order trends from peers (KLAC, AMAT) for confirmation of AI capex strength

discoveries.name: Photronics, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors make no mention of AI as a competitive threat. Business risks center on semiconductor industry cyclicality, customer concentration, and tariffs. Photomasks are a physical input to every chip manufactured.
discoveries.thesis: Photomasks are required for every semiconductor design release; AI chip proliferation increases design activity (Photronics explicitly notes demand depends on design activity, not just sales volume). A Slack AI integration has no mechanism to reduce photomask demand. The sharp drop is pure semi-sympathy noise.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued strong design-release activity at leading foundries; capex spending of $330M for FY26 proceeding on plan.
discoveries.what_kills: A major customer (top 2 = 29% of revenue) cutting orders; consolidation in semiconductor customer base reducing design diversity.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PLAB sits in the semi-cap sympathy basket; retail conflates 'AI news = sell tech' without checking whether the company's product has any AI substitution risk.
research_pointers: Check recent design-release commentary from TSMC and Samsung
Monitor FPD (flat panel display) demand which is a secondary revenue driver

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors explicitly state 'infrastructure investments in artificial intelligence (AI) have increased substantially, which is driving significant demand increases in the Data Center Computing market.' AI is framed as a tailwind, not a threat.
discoveries.thesis: AEIS sells power conversion to both semiconductor fabs and AI data centers. The 10-K explicitly identifies AI as a demand driver they are scaling capacity to meet. A Slack AI integration is mechanically backwards as a bear catalyst — more enterprise AI deployment → more data-center power demand → more AEIS. The drop is pure sympathy.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued data-center capex commentary from hyperscalers; AEIS guiding to capacity expansion completion.
discoveries.what_kills: Evidence of data-center buildout pause; semiconductor capex cycle peak signals.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: AEIS is in the AI-adjacent industrials basket; on AI-lab news days, retail dumps anything tagged 'AI-exposed' without distinguishing beneficiaries from victims.
research_pointers: Read Data Center Computing segment growth in latest quarterly print
Check hyperscaler capex guides (META, MSFT, GOOG, AMZN)

discoveries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss AI as both a growth investment area (DOCN's own AI/ML offerings) and a competitive consideration, but the company is positioned as an AI infrastructure provider for SMBs/developers, not an enterprise collaboration tool. No language identifying Slack-embedded AI teammates as a competitive threat.
discoveries.thesis: Claude Tag in Slack is an enterprise productivity feature; DOCN sells cloud infrastructure to developers and SMBs. There's no product-overlap mechanism. The five-day drop is already extended, suggesting the move has multiple ingredients (general cloud sympathy on AI capex peak fears), but today's incremental drop on Claude Tag specifically is unjustified.
discoveries.what_confirms: DOCN's own AI/ML revenue line growing; SMB cloud demand metrics stable.
discoveries.what_kills: Multi-day 5-day weakness suggests something else may be in play — check for any DOCN-specific guidance issues or hyperscaler price-cut announcements that could pressure DOCN's pricing.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a name is down sharply over five days AND today, the marginal sympathy-fade may be small relative to the standalone weakness — confidence should be tempered.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DOCN gets lumped into the 'SaaS productivity disrupted by AI' bucket on any AI-lab news, despite serving a different layer of the stack (IaaS for developers).
research_pointers: Investigate the five-day weakness — check for any guidance pre-announce or analyst downgrade
Review DOCN's AI/ML product traction (GPU droplets, AI agents)
Compare DOCN's customer acquisition trends versus hyperscaler SMB segments

discoveries.name: Cognex Corporation
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors acknowledge that 'advancements in the availability of sophisticated AI algorithms and open-source machine vision platforms may lower barriers to enter our market' — AI is flagged as a longer-term competitive consideration, but their products embed AI and serve deterministic factory-floor use cases.
discoveries.thesis: CGNX serves industrial QA on factory floors requiring deterministic latency and precision. A Slack AI teammate has zero overlap with industrial machine vision. While CGNX's filings do mention AI as a long-term competitive risk, today's selloff on an enterprise-collaboration news item is a sympathy fade, not a credible threat materializing.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued industrial automation capex; factory-floor design wins in EV/logistics.
discoveries.what_kills: Evidence of open-source vision platforms taking share at factory-floor accounts; weakness in the consumer electronics segment.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: CGNX is in the industrial-AI sympathy basket; on AI news days, retail dumps anything with 'vision' in the product description.
research_pointers: Check end-market exposure (consumer electronics versus logistics versus EV battery)
Monitor competing open-source vision platform adoption signals

skipped.reason: Indirect threat — edge-AI inference SoCs face real competitive pressure from larger players (NVIDIA, Qualcomm) explicitly named in Risk Factors; the panic narrative has more substance than a clean sympathy fade and confidence in 'unjustified' calibration is low.
Positive move (+7.14%) — not a sympathy-fade buy candidate; no panic to fade.
Positive move (+7.09%) on high volume — market is correctly identifying VRNS as an AI beneficiary; no panic to fade.
Positive move (+3.87%) — not a panic-fade candidate.
Positive move (+3.52%) — not a panic-fade candidate.
Positive move (+3.49%) — not a panic-fade candidate.

_trigger.headline: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack

The feature captures organizational context and institutional knowledge through ongoing message monitoring, positioning it as both a productivity tool and a strategic play to deepen enterprise workflow integration.
